text:
Market today: Mixed signals for your tech/chip book. TSMC's record revenue is a positive for chips, but surging oil prices due to geopolitical tensions and SK Hynix's leverage exposure could create broader market headwinds.

Your holdings: SOXL is down 24.46% and SPAL is down 27.71%. Both are leveraged ETFs and are currently below their stated stops. Trading these on margin amplifies these drops significantly, requiring immediate attention.

Watch: Keep a close eye on TSMC's earnings report on Thursday for further chip sector direction. Also, monitor oil prices and any escalation in geopolitical tensions, as they can quickly sour overall market mood.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk in your leveraged positions, especially with key chip news and geopolitical events unfolding.
plays: Consider trimming SOXL (SELL, -24.46%) immediately, as it is below its 200 stop. Leveraged ETFs decay and amplify losses on margin.
Consider trimming SPAL (NEW, -27.71%) immediately, as it is below its 21.38 stop. This leveraged ETF moves fast and amplifies losses.
No new buys today - hold and watch. Focus on risk management given current market conditions and existing drawdowns.
